component.name: Fan Control
component.summary: Next-generation KDE fan control for Linux desktops

description.p: KDE Fan Control is a next-generation fan control experience for the KDE desktop. A privileged Rust daemon owns hardware writes, applies per-fan PID policies, and exposes one DBus control surface to the GUI, tray, and CLI.

The result is modern, reviewable fan management: discover hardware, stage changes in a draft, validate them before applying, and keep controlled fans on a fail-safe path that returns them to maximum speed if the daemon stops unexpectedly.

ul.li: Per-fan PID control with configurable gains, limits, and cadence
Automatic hardware discovery from Linux hwmon sysfs
KDE-native GUI with overview, inventory, fan detail pages, and wizard setup
System tray integration with status notifications and quick visibility into alerts
Draft, validate, and apply lifecycle for reviewable configuration changes
Auto-tune proposals, multi-sensor aggregation, and friendly names
Polkit-authenticated write operations with read-open DBus access
Fail-safe fallback and degraded-fan recovery for safer runtime behavior

screenshot.caption: Overview dashboard with live fan state, temperatures, RPM, and output
Inventory page for discovered fans and sensors
Fan detail page for tuning control mode, sources, target temperature, and PID gains
Wizard-based setup for guided fan enrollment
System tray popover showing degraded state and active alerts

description.p: Fix critical bug where applying any draft change caused all previously-managed fans to lose their PID control settings and revert to maximum speed.
Fix polkit authorization that prevented GUI unlock.
Packaging follow-up release that fixes installed application icon naming and refreshes bundled product diagrams.
